rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER LOGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint For Failure To Serve Defendants Within Three Years From Filing Complaint; **First Appearance Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, BARBARA SMITH NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT FOR FAILURE TO SERVE DEFENDANTS WITHIN THREE YEARS FROM FILING COMPLAINT; **FIRST APPEARANCE FEES PAID** Hearing Required to address (1) whether SFHA offices were closed to public at any time since March 2020 and for how long; (2) if the offices were closed to the public, was there a procedure for service established; and (3) how this procedure was communicated to the public. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AE RETAIL WEST, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF AE RETAIL WEST, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE is GRANTED. Counsel to prepare an order. No opposition filed. The Court notes that the proposed order submitted with the moving papers has no cover page and cannot be processed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HARVEST SMALL BUSINESS FINANCE, LLC VS. ELEN MEDER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Verified First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT ELEN MEDER NOTICE OF MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND VERIFIED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing Required. Plaintiff to file a Proof of Service for the Summons and Complaint and present the process server at the hearing (in-person or via Zoom with video capability) if Plaintiff asserts that service was effectuated on November 30, 2023. If Plaintiff does not contend that November 30, 2023 delivery of Summons and Complaint constitutes valid service (or part thereof), Plaintiff may state so at the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: COLLEEN S. O'NEAL VS. CATHLEEN PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses As To The Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Sanctions As To Defendant James Peros
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF COLLEEN O'NEAL PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES AS TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND SANCTIONS AS TO DEFENDANT JAMES PEROS is granted, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to the Form Interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $690. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
